svg.text: Adam, Dan, Frank, Jack, Jake, James
MALE — LONG (6 letters or more) · ask them to shorten if a nickname exists
Abby, Amy, Anna, Ava, Carol, Dana, Grace
Kate / Katie, Mary, Paige, Sara / Sarah, Tara
FEMALE — LONG (6 letters or more) · ask for full name · Meagan excluded (spelling varies)
Abigail, Amanda, Ashley, Danielle, Hannah, Jackie
SECOND VOWEL TIEBREAKER · Female Long K–Z · First vowel A (most crowded cell)
Y NAMES · visible hesitation on the first vowel question is the tell
Male: Ryan, Kyle, Tyler · Female: Lynn, Sydney, Crystal · If they pause to decide whether Y counts as a vowel — it is Y.
HOW TO READ THIS IN PERFORMANCE
1 Gender → 2 Short / Long → 3 Alphabet half → 4 First vowel (Fruit to Fang) → 5 Second vowel if needed
Example: woman · long · K–Z · first vowel E → Melissa, Heather, Rebecca → call Melissa (most common)
If wrong: "You're not thinking of Melissa, are you? Good — that's the name I've gotten the last three times. I think this is someone whose name you hear less often these days."
